message.content:
{
  "analysis": "The autotune run finished but produced only a 0-byte `/app/work/model_aut2.ftz` and no valid `.bin`, so `/app/model.bin` has not been created. The terminal is back at a clean prompt. We need a clean manual fallback that trains, evaluates, quantizes if necessary, and saves a valid model to `/app/model.bin` under 150MB with local test accuracy >=0.62. To avoid terminal pollution from interactive polling, I will run one foreground script that manages the whole workflow with logging and bounded waits.",
  "plan": "Create and run `/app/work/final_train.sh`. It will kill stray fastText processes, clean stale artifacts, train a supervised fastText model on `/app/work/train.txt` with a balanced configuration, poll for completion inside the script, evaluate on `/app/work/test.txt`, quantize with retraining if the raw model is over 135MB, re-evaluate the quantized model, and copy the first qualifying model to `/app/model.bin`. It will print clear status and final verification at the end.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > /app/work/final_train.sh <<'SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
set -u
FSTEXT=/opt/fastText/fasttext
TRAIN=/app/work/train.txt
TEST=/app/work/test.txt
BASE=/app/work/model_final
FINAL=/app/model.bin
MAX_SIZE=150000000
QUANT_TRIGGER=135000000
MIN_ACC=0.62
SEED=42

echo "=== final_train.sh start $(date -Iseconds) ==="

# Kill stray fasttext supervised processes
for p in /proc/[0-9]*; do
  cmd=$(tr '\0' ' ' < "$p/cmdline" 2>/dev/null)
  case "$cmd" in
    *fasttext*) echo "killing ${p##*/}: $cmd"; kill -KILL "${p##*/}" 2>/dev/null || true;;
  esac
done
sleep 2

# Clean stale artifacts
rm -f "$BASE".bin "$BASE".vec "$BASE".ftz /app/work/train_final.log /app/work/train_final.pid /app/work/eval_final.txt /app/work/quantize_final.log /app/work/model_final_quant.ftz
rm -f /app/work/model_aut2.bin /app/work/model_aut2.vec /app/work/model_aut2.ftz /app/work/autotune2.log /app/work/autotune2.pid /app/work/eval_aut2.txt
rm -f "$FINAL"

echo "=== Data check ==="
wc -l "$TRAIN" "$TEST"
df -h /app /app/work | tail -5

echo "=== Training supervised model ==="
nohup "$FSTEXT" supervised \
  -input "$TRAIN" \
  -output "$BASE" \
  -dim 100 \
  -epoch 25 \
  -lr 0.7 \
  -wordNgrams 2 \
  -minn 3 \
  -maxn 5 \
  -bucket 200000 \
  -minCount 2 \
  -loss softmax \
  -thread "$(nproc)" \
  -seed "$SEED" \
  > /app/work/train_final.log 2>&1 &
train_pid=$!
echo "$train_pid" > /app/work/train_final.pid
echo "train_pid=$train_pid"

# Poll for completion up to 25 minutes
for i in $(seq 1 50); do
  if [ -d "/proc/$train_pid" ]; then status=running; else status=finished; fi
  size=$(stat -c %s "$BASE.bin" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  printf '(%s/50) status=%s bin_size=%s ' "$i" "$status" "$size"
  tail -1 /app/work/train_final.log 2>/dev/null || true
  if [ "$status" = finished ] && [ "$size" -gt 0 ]; then echo 'training_done'; break; fi
  if [ "$status" = finished ] && [ "$size" -eq 0 ]; then echo 'training_finished_no_model'; break; fi
  sleep 30
done

if [ ! -s "$BASE.bin" ]; then
  echo "ERROR: no valid raw model produced"
  tail -40 /app/work/train_final.log 2>/dev/null || true
  exit 1
fi

rm -f "$BASE.vec"
RAW_SIZE=$(stat -c %s "$BASE.bin")
echo "raw_model_size=$RAW_SIZE"

echo "=== Evaluating raw model ==="
"$FSTEXT" test "$BASE.bin" "$TEST" 2>&1 | tee /app/work/eval_final.txt
RAW_ACC=$(awk '/^P@1/ {print $3+0}' /app/work/eval_final.txt 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
echo "raw_accuracy=$RAW_ACC"

if [ "$RAW_SIZE" -lt "$MAX_SIZE" ] && awk -v min="$MIN_ACC" '/^P@1/ {exit !($3+0 >= min)}' /app/work/eval_final.txt; then
  cp "$BASE.bin" "$FINAL"
  echo "copied raw model to $FINAL"
else
  echo "raw model not acceptable; raw_size=$RAW_SIZE raw_accuracy=$RAW_ACC"
  if [ "$RAW_SIZE" -gt "$QUANT_TRIGGER" ] || ! awk -v min="$MIN_ACC" '/^P@1/ {exit !($3+0 >= min)}' /app/work/eval_final.txt; then
    echo "=== Quantizing with retrain ==="
    "$FSTEXT" quantize \
      -input "$BASE.bin" \
      -output "$BASE" \
      -qnorm -retrain -cutoff 100000 \
      -thread "$(nproc)" \
      > /app/work/quantize_final.log 2>&1
    echo "quantize_exit=$?"
    tail -30 /app/work/quantize_final.log 2>/dev/null || true
    if [ ! -s "$BASE.ftz" ]; then
      echo "ERROR: quantization did not produce $BASE.ftz"
      exit 1
    fi
    QUANT_SIZE=$(stat -c %s "$BASE.ftz")
    echo "quantized_size=$QUANT_SIZE"
    echo "=== Evaluating quantized model ==="
    "$FSTEXT" test "$BASE.ftz" "$TEST" 2>&1 | tee /app/work/eval_final.txt
    QUANT_ACC=$(awk '/^P@1/ {print $3+0}' /app/work/eval_final.txt 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    echo "quantized_accuracy=$QUANT_ACC"
    if [ "$QUANT_SIZE" -lt "$MAX_SIZE" ] && awk -v min="$MIN_ACC" '/^P@1/ {exit !($3+0 >= min)}' /app/work/eval_final.txt; then
      cp "$BASE.ftz" "$FINAL"
      echo "copied quantized model to $FINAL"
    else
      echo "ERROR: quantized model not acceptable; quantized_size=$QUANT_SIZE quantized_accuracy=$QUANT_ACC"
      exit 1
    fi
  else
    echo "ERROR: raw model failed accuracy but is not large enough for quantization fallback"
    exit 1
  fi
fi

echo "=== Final verification ==="
ls -lh "$FINAL"
echo "final_size_bytes=$(stat -c %s "$FINAL")"
"$FSTEXT" test "$FINAL" "$TEST" 2>&1 | tee /app/work/eval_final_check.txt
echo "=== final_train.sh done $(date -Iseconds) ==="
SCRIPT
chmod +x /app/work/final_train.sh
/app/work/final_train.sh
",
      "duration": 60.0
    }
  ]
}
